topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing financial assistance and support services for veterans, particularly in areas such as burial expenses and education benefits.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to cover the burial and funeral expenses of a deceased veteran
Prohibits any deduction from the burial allowance because of a veteran's assets
Document 2: Sets $25 million as the maximum amount per fiscal year for reimbursement to states and local agencies administering veterans' education benefits
Document 3: Authorizes a state or state agency to use assistance provided under the Hardest Hit Fund program to demolish blighted structures

topic.name: Medicare Policy Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming Medicare policies to improve access to healthcare services and reduce costs for beneficiaries.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to increase the type of originating sites allowed to offer telehealth services
Authorize the Director of the Office for the Advancement of Telehealth to award grants to demonstrate how telehealth technologies can be used to expand access to health care services.
Document 2: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to negotiate with pharmaceutical manufacturers the prices
for covered part D drugs for part D eligible individuals.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to deny the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) authority to restrict or eliminate a Medicare beneficiary's option of electing to have diabetic testing supplies delivered
to him or her by retail community pharmacies.

topic.name: Education Funding and Support Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative initiatives aimed at improving educational outcomes through funding and support for teachers, students, and after-school programs.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive matching grants to partnerships for the development of national model after-school interdisciplinary education programs.
Gives grant and subgrant priority to programs targeting a high percentage of impoverished, struggling, or rural students and their families.
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to expand the loan forgiveness available to public elementary and secondary school teachers.
Raises to $25,000 the amount of the FFEL or DL outstanding that may be forgiven after a public school teacher's fifth complete school year of teaching.
Document 3: Directs the Department of Education to maintain a consumer-tested online counseling tool that provides required entrance counseling to a student who is a first-time federal student loan borrower.

topic.name: U.S. Tariff Regulations
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures related to the modification and suspension of tariffs under the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: Directs the International Trade Commission (ITC) to propose to Congress temporary duty suspensions or duty reductions
Prohibits the ITC from recommending any suspension or reduction in duty if certain conditions are met.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic synthetic staple fiber.
Document 3: This bill amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend for 2016 through 2025 duty-free treatment of certain textile goods imported into the United States from Morocco.

topic.name: Veterans Healthcare and Compensation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving healthcare access and compensation for veterans, particularly those with service-related disabilities.

Document 1: amends the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act of 2014
ensure that veterans enrolled in the health care system... are able to receive health care at VA medical facilities
Document 2: defines 'combat with the enemy' for purposes of proof of service-connection for veterans' disability compensation
Document 3: reduces the level of difficulty required in the use of prostheses by veterans
authorizes additional veterans' disability compensation for service-disabled veterans

topic.name: Oil and Gas Development Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the exploration, production, and regulation of oil and natural gas resources in the United States.

Document 1: Expresses the sense of Congress that the National Petroleum Reserve (NPR) in Alaska remains explicitly designated to provide oil and natural gas resources to the United States
requires the mandatory program of competitive leasing of oil and gas in the NPR to include at least one lease sale annually
Document 2: Prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from authorizing any new lease for exploration or production of oil or natural gas unless the lessee certifies for each existing lease that the lessee has diligently developed the lands
Establishes a civil penalty for noncompliance with this Act

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Reform
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at regulating immigration, refugee admission, and the rights of immigrants in the United States.

Document 1: requires that the U.S. refugee admission of a covered alien...
the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shall ensure that such person has passed a lie detector test
Document 2: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996
restores provisions of law amended by such provision as if it had not been enacted
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to provide that an alien who fails to appear at a removal proceeding shall be fined
not less than one year and not more than four years (or 10 years if deportable under smuggling, security, criminal, or document falsification grounds)

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: The topic focuses on the assessment and accountability of foreign countries' programs related to electromagnetic pulse weapons.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness Area Designation
topic.description: The documents discuss the designation and management of wilderness areas and conservation lands in various regions of the United States.

Document 1: Designates specified Bureau of Land Management (BLM) and National Forest System lands in California as wilderness areas
Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to study nonmotorized recreation trail opportunities
Document 2: Designates specified wilderness areas in New Mexico and administered by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) as wilderness
Establishes certain BLM-managed lands in the county as the Organ Mountains and Desert Peaks National Conservation Areas
Document 3: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as wilderness and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System
Prohibits any use of motorized vehicles and other motorized machinery in the Area

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and measures aimed at improving the welfare and protection of children, particularly in contexts of adoption, domestic violence, and online exploitation.

Document 1: Strengthening and Finding Families for Children Act
Amends part E (Foster Care and Adoption Assistance) of title IV of the Social Security Act
Document 2: Directs the Commissioner of Social Security to pay the fees charged by any governmental authority associated with obtaining the birth certificate or state residence identification card
Document 3: Securing Adolescents From Exploitation-Online Act of 2007
expands the reporting requirements of electronic communication and remote computing service providers with respect to violations of child sexual exploitation and pornography laws

topic.name: Mental Health and Support Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving mental health services, housing support, and addressing chronic homelessness through coordinated care and funding.

Document 1: require the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to design national strategies
services designed to help individuals and families make progress toward self-sufficiency
Document 2: authorize the use of specified funds by state and local governments
support services for individuals released from inpatient or residential institutions
Document 3: reauthorize appropriations for programs established under the Combating Autism Act
requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to submit a progress report on activities related to autism spectrum disorders

topic.name: Drug Policy Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to investigational drugs, addressing opioid abuse, and reforming marijuana prohibition.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the sponsor of an 'investigational drug' to submit to the FDA and make available to the public the sponsor's policy on requests for expanded access.
Requires an investigational drug sponsor to explain a denied request for expanded access to the person who made the request.
Document 2: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services to convene an interagency task force to develop best practices for pain management and prescription of pain medication.
Limits an individual's civil liability for harm caused by the emergency administration of an opioid overdose reversal drug.

topic.name: Ecosystem Restoration and Water Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at restoring ecosystems and managing water resources across various regions in the United States.

Document 1: Restoring the Everglades, an American Legacy Act of 2007
ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality
Document 2: San Gabriel Watershed Restoration Act
feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration, flood control, water quality control, water supply storage
Document 3: South Orange County Recycled Water Enhancement Act
design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility and recycled water system

topic.name: FHA Mortgage Regulation
topic.description: Regulations and reforms related to the Federal Housing Administration's mortgage insurance and underwriting practices.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to require specified mortgagees to indemnify HUD for payment of a mortgage insurance claim
Establishes within the Federal Housing Administration (FHA) a Deputy Assistant Secretary for Risk Management and Regulatory Affairs
Document 2: Amends the National Housing Act to remove the aggregate and insurance benefit limitations on the number of home equity conversion (reverse) mortgages for elderly homeowners
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to establish and collect additional annual premium payments for the first 11 years of the term of an insured mortgage
Revises conditions for the Secretary's exercise of authority to require indemnification for an insurance loss claim paid on a mortgage

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislative efforts aimed at the conservation and sustainability of coral reef ecosystems.

Document 1: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act
authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force
Document 2: direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy
authorizes the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships promoting coral reef ecosystem conservation
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems
authorizes the Secretary to conduct activities, including with local, regional, or international programs and partners, to conserve coral reef ecosystems

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating and supporting agricultural practices and crop management in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the U.S. Troop Readiness, Veterans' Care, Katrina Recovery, and Iraq Accountability Appropriations Act, 2007 to eliminate the requirement that forage producers must participate in the insurance pilot program.
crop disaster and livestock assistance.
Document 2: Farm-to-Fuel Investment Act of 2007 - Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to designate as bioenergy cropsheds areas that are physically located within a 50-mile radius.
offer to enter into contracts with producers on a farm in a bioenergy cropshed to provide bioenergy crop transition assistance.
Document 3: Farming Flexibility Act of 2007 - Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002, with respect to direct and counter-cyclical payments.
permit a producer to reduce a covered commodity base acreage by electing to plant such acres with fruits or vegetables for processing.

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Climate Adaptation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting renewable fuels and adapting water systems to climate change impacts.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program
Defines 'next generation biofuel' to mean renewable fuel derived from renewable biomass.
Document 2: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish and provide funding for a program of directed and applied research
Research areas to include water quality impacts and solutions.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to direct the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a fuel emission baseline
Requires the average lifecycle GHG emissions of the aggregate quantity for 2012 to be at least 3% below the 2007 average.

topic.name: Cybersecurity and Privacy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on federal laws and amendments aimed at enhancing cybersecurity measures and protecting personal privacy in the digital space.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to permit a video tape service provider to obtain a consumer's consent to disclose personally identifiable information.
disclose personally identifiable information concerning the consumer.
Document 2: Cyber Privacy Fortification Act of 2015
Provides criminal penalties for intentional failures to provide required notices of a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Information Security Management Act of 2002 (FISMA) to reestablish the oversight authority of the Director of the Office of Management and Budget.

Requires the Secretary to develop and oversee implementation of operational directives requiring agencies to implement the Director's standards and guidelines for safeguarding federal information and systems.

topic.name: Financial Crime Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing financial crimes such as money laundering, identity theft, and tax-related fraud.

Document 1: Combating Money Laundering, Terrorist Financing, and Counterfeiting Act of 2011
Prohibits knowingly conducting a monetary transaction involving the proceeds of unlawful activity
Document 2: Tax Refund Theft Prevention Act of 2014
impose a fine and/or prison term on any person who willfully misappropriates another person's taxpayer identity
Document 3: Taxpayer Protection Act of 2007
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to notify a taxpayer of any unauthorized use of such taxpayer's identity

topic.name: Banking Regulation Reforms
topic.description: Legislation aimed at modifying banking regulations to provide relief and exemptions for smaller banking institutions and enhance transparency in federal monetary policy.

Document 1: Community Bank Access to Capital Act of 2015
exempting community banks from regulations implementing 'Basel III'
Document 2: Volcker Rule Relief Act of 2015
exempts from the Volcker Rule any banking entity with total consolidated assets of $10 billion or less
Document 3: Federal Reserve Accountability and Transparency Act of 2014
requires the Chairman of the Federal Reserve Board to testify before certain congressional committees

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: These documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at the conservation and management of wildlife and their habitats.

doc_id_1: funding for conservation of migratory waterfowl
doc_id_2: state management plan for Rocky Mountain gray wolf populations

topic.name: Legislative Measures and Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures and amendments related to national security, aviation safety, and water resource management.

Document 1: Rewards for Justice program authorized under the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956
offer a reward to any individual who furnishes information leading to the arrest or conviction in any country of any individual for committing... kidnapping and murder
Document 2: Small Airplane Revitalization Act of 2013
Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to issue a final rule to advance the safety and continued development of small airplanes
Document 3: Amends the East Bench Irrigation District Water Contract Extension Act
to permit the Secretary of the Interior to extend the contract for water services between the United States and the East Bench Irrigation District

topic.name: Transportation Regulatory Acts
topic.description: Legislation focused on regulating various aspects of transportation, including aircraft noise compliance, rail project funding, and emergency exemptions for motor carriers.

Document 1: Silent Skies Act of 2013 - Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to enter into a competitive cooperative agreement
requires the Secretary to request the International Civil Aviation Organization to add to its Work Programme
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to make allocations for capital investment grants for FY2015-FY2024
allows for the use of any alternative analysis or environmental impact report relating the Project
Document 3: Declares that a covered emergency exemption from federal motor carrier safety regulations shall remain in effect until May 31, 2014
defines 'covered emergency exemption' as one issued or extended to effect regulatory relief for commercial motor vehicle operations

topic.name: STEM Education and Grants
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation aimed at promoting and funding STEM education initiatives and research through competitive grants.

Document 1: Requires the National Science Foundation (NSF) to award competitive grants for basic research on innovative approaches to improve health care information systems
establish multidisciplinary Centers for Informatics Research for conducting cutting-edge, multidisciplinary research
Document 2: directs the Department of Education to award competitive grants for science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) elementary and secondary school programs
support STEM workforce training and career preparation for secondary school students
Document 3: amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Department of Education to award grants to national and regional intermediaries
The STEM programs must primarily serve students who are traditionally underrepresented in STEM field careers

topic.name: Retirement Savings Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing retirement savings options and financial literacy for individuals and families.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow funds paid to an individual to aid in the pursuit of graduate or postdoctoral study or research to be saved in an Individual Retirement Account (IRA)
permits the funds to be considered compensation for purposes of current law provisions that limit annual deductible IRA contributions
Document 2: Establishes a KIDS Account Fund in the Treasury
Provides for distributions for higher education expenses
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to employer-established simple individual retirement accounts (IRAs) and pension plans
allow small employers a new tax credit for the cost of adopting safe harbor requirements for secure deferral arrangements

topic.name: Women's Business Ownership Programs
topic.description: Legislative initiatives aimed at supporting and enhancing the success of women entrepreneurs through training, funding, and policy improvements.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to direct the Office of Women's Business Ownership within the Small Business Administration (SBA) to address issues concerning specified disciplines required for starting, operating, and increasing a small business.
Authorizes the SBA Administrator to provide financial assistance to qualifying entities to conduct projects designed to provide training and counseling meeting the needs of women business owners.
Document 2: Amends the Women's Business Ownership Act of 1988 to direct the National Women's Business Council to conduct at least one annual study that evaluates problems hindering the success of women entrepreneurs.
Revises matching funds requirements for such projects.

topic.name: Indian Tribal Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts aimed at enhancing health care, forensic services, and land management for Indian tribes.

Document 1: awards grants to states, local governments, and Indian tribes to establish new criminal forensic laboratories
give priority in awarding such grants to agencies in jurisdictions with the largest backlogs of criminal forensic examinations.
Document 2: amends the Indian Health Care Improvement Act to revise requirements for health care programs and services for Indians
authorizes grants to urban Indian organizations for health information technology, telemedicine services development, and related infrastructure.
Document 3: takes specified federal land within the Park into trust for the Quileute Indian Tribe
prohibits gaming on lands taken into trust for the Tribe pursuant to this Act.

topic.name: Social Security Reform
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at modifying Social Security benefits and cost-of-living adjustments for retirees.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to prescribe special rules for the determination of taxable wages and self-employment income above the contribution and benefit base after 2013.
Directs the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) to prepare and publish the Consumer Price Index for Elderly Consumers (CPI-EC).
Document 2: This bill amends title II of the Social Security Act to revise cost-of-living computation requirements.
Annual minimum and maximum cost-of-living increases for Social Security beneficiaries shall range between 3% and 4%.
Document 3: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to increase the normal retirement age by specified graduated stages to age 70.
Revises requirements for computation of the age increase factor.

topic.name: Regulatory and Tax Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative reforms aimed at improving regulatory processes and tax incentives for businesses and workforce development.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend tax credits and expensing allowances for businesses.
Amends the Workforce Investment Act to prioritize industry-recognized training programs.
Document 2: Modifies rule making requirements and procedures of federal agencies under the Regulatory Flexibility Act.
Agencies are required to consider economic impacts on small entities.
Document 3: Requires federal agencies to conduct periodic reviews of major rules to assess costs and benefits.
Defines 'major rule' based on significant economic impact or adverse effects.

topic.name: Iran Sanctions and Policy
topic.description: Legislative measures and policies aimed at addressing Iran's nuclear ambitions, support for terrorism, and human rights violations.

Document 1: Sets forth congressional findings regarding Iranian support for terrorism, Iran's ballistic missile fleet, and Iran's nuclear program.
Directs the President to impose sanctions against persons importing Iranian goods or services other than news publications into the United States.
Document 2: Prohibits or imposes strict conditions on the maintaining of accounts by foreign financial institutions that conduct significant arms sales to the government of Bashar al Assad.
Supports efforts for a peaceful resolution of the conflict in Syria and the establishment of an inclusive representative form of government.
Document 3: Prescribes requirements for sanctions with respect to Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps and related entities.
The bill expresses the sense of Congress with respect to human rights in Iran and Iran's ballistic missile program.

topic.name: Housing Assistance Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at improving housing access and support for low-income individuals and vulnerable populations, including the homeless and foster youth.

Document 1: amends the United States Housing Act of 1937
require local governments or other HUD-approved entities to make inspections and rent determinations
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to include local educational agency homeless liaisons
assisting homeless and foster care youth in accessing and completing postsecondary education
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make grants to state housing credit agencies
finance the construction or acquisition and rehabilitation of qualified low-income buildings

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating the use of public funds in political campaigns and contributions.

Document 1: Prohibits the use of federal funds received by any entity to advocate the election or defeat of a political candidate
Exempts from this ban the use of funds by an entity for an activity specifically authorized by federal law
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to terminate taxpayer checkoffs to finance presidential election campaigns
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to transfer any amounts remaining in the Fund after 2009 to the general fund of the Treasury
Document 3: Allows a $200 tax credit for contributions to a candidate for federal elective public office
Provides a tax deduction for political contributions up to $600

topic.name: Credit Card Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumers and small businesses in relation to credit card practices and disclosures.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to cover any qualified small business as a consumer
Prohibits a creditor from discriminating against any business concern having 50 or fewer employees
Document 2: Prohibits imposition of an interest charge upon debt paid on time and in full
Requires the primary federal regulator of a card issuer to conduct annual audits of the credit card operations

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating various aspects of telecommunications, including device operation, broadcast signal transmission, and restrictions on unsolicited communication.

Document 1: Interference Protection for Existing Television Band Devices Act of 2007
protect from harmful interference incumbent certified low-power auxiliary devices
Document 2: Satellite Consumers' Right to Local Channels Act
require each satellite carrier providing secondary transmissions in the local market
Document 3: Quell Unnecessary, Intentional, and Encroaching Telephone Calls Act of 2014
prohibit a person within the United States from knowingly initiating a commercial robocall

topic.name: Military Personnel Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring the mental health, pay, and operational readiness of military personnel and their families.

Document 1: post-deployment mental health assessment
requires annual reports from the Secretary to Congress
Document 2: directs the President to submit a certification regarding end strength reduction
prohibits end strength reduction beyond specified limits
Document 3: requires the Secretary of the Treasury to transfer amounts necessary for pay
pay obligation to take priority over all other government obligations

topic.name: Consumer Product Safety and Health
topic.description: Legislation focused on ensuring the safety and health standards of consumer products and housing through regulations and tax incentives.

Document 1: Establishes the Financial Product Safety Commission to promulgate consumer financial product safety rules
conduct continuing studies and investigations of consumer financial products industry practices
Document 2: amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow new tax credits for lead hazard reduction activity costs
the credit for such costs is generally limited to $5,000 for any eligible dwelling
Document 3: Requires a cleaning product to bear a label listing each of its ingredients
increases penalties for violations of FHSA

topic.name: Labor Rights and Employment
topic.description: This topic encompasses the rights and regulations surrounding collective bargaining and employment conditions for veterans and public safety employees under federal and state laws.

Document 1: establish and maintain an Internet website to publicly disclose information concerning the number of veterans employed under federal contracts
procurement of personal property and nonpersonal services
Document 2: Provides collective bargaining rights for public safety officers employed by states or local governments
granting public safety employees the right to form and join a labor organization
Document 3: Repeals specified exceptions to rights of certain Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) employees to engage in collective bargaining
final decision of the VA with respect to the review of an adverse personnel action against a VA employee

topic.name: Legal Protections Against Foreign Judgments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legal measures and acts aimed at protecting U.S. citizens and entities from the enforcement of foreign judgments that may undermine domestic legal standards, particularly in matters of national security and free speech.

Document 1: prohibit the use of funds made available to the Department of Defense to transfer... any individual detained at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay... unless DOD certifies to Congress that the individual no longer poses a threat
amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2016
Document 2: Makes unenforceable by a federal or state agency, department, or court any judgment of a foreign court or agency...
authorizes a U.S. plaintiff... to file a civil action for the amount of such damages against the foreign defendant
Document 3: Prohibits a domestic court from recognizing or enforcing a foreign judgment for defamation unless the domestic court determines that...
specifies circumstances for removal by a defendant to the appropriate U.S. district court

topic.name: Education Policy and Grants
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to education that involve grant funding and requirements for environmental literacy, campus justice, and health services in schools.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require states...to develop environmental literacy plans
Authorizes the Secretary to award competitive matching grants to nonprofit organizations...for activities to improve and support environmental education.
Document 2: Amends the General Education Provisions Act to require an institution of postsecondary education to disclose...the final results of any disciplinary proceeding
Current law states that such disclosure is not prohibited.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to make matching demonstration grants to local educational agencies...for the purpose of reducing such ratio
Gives grant priority to LEAs...that demonstrate the greatest need for new or additional nursing services for their students.

topic.name: Election Transparency Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing transparency and public involvement in the electoral process through various requirements for redistricting, vote tallying, and poll information dissemination.

Document 1: Requires states to carry out congressional redistricting in accordance with a process under which members of the public are informed of redistricting proposals via the Internet
solicit the input of members of the public in its work to develop initial congressional redistricting plans
Document 2: Requires the appropriate election official, upon the closing of the polls at each polling place, to announce the vote orally
post a copy of the poll tape reflecting the totals from each voting machine in the polling place
Document 3: require states to meet specified standards for transparent and accurate tabulation of votes
mandatory hand recount in case of unresolved discrepancy

topic.name: Firearm Legislation and Terrorism
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal laws related to the regulation of firearms, particularly focusing on concealed carry reciprocity and measures to prevent firearms access to individuals involved in terrorism.

Document 1: Respecting States Rights and Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act of 2009
provides for reciprocity for the carrying of certain concealed firearms in different states
Document 2: Denying Firearms and Explosives to Dangerous Terrorists Act of 2007
grant the Attorney General the authority to deny the transfer of firearms
Document 3: Denying Firearms and Explosives to Dangerous Terrorists Act of 2011
authorize the Attorney General to revoke firearms and explosives licenses

topic.name: Aviation Security and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation and security measures within the aviation industry, including airport screening and funding for aviation infrastructure.

Document 1: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (Transportation Security Administration [TSA]) to issue regulations for airport perimeter screening.
Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to hire necessary passenger and baggage screeners.
Document 2: Amends federal transportation law to extend funding for airport planning and development.
Extends authority of the Secretary of Transportation to incur obligations for public-use airports.
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of the FAA to block aircraft registration numbers in public display.
Allows information to be available to government agencies upon request.

topic.name: Food Safety and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring the safety and proper labeling of food products and agricultural practices in the United States.

Document 1: prohibits shipping, transporting, offering for sale, selling, or purchasing a genetically altered finfish
enforce penalties for violations of this Act
Document 2: require menstrual cups, menstrual pads, tampons, and therapeutic vaginal douche apparatuses to include a list of ingredients on the label
Document 3: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish standards for good manufacturing practices for the minimal processing of produce
establish general standards for good agricultural practices for the production of raw agricultural commodities

topic.name: Native Hawaiian and Indigenous Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legal recognition, rights, and governance of Native Hawaiian and other Indigenous communities in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes the U.S. Office for Native Hawaiian Affairs within the Office of the Secretary of the Interior.
Recognizes the right of the Native Hawaiian people to adopt organic governing documents.
Document 3: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to permit the Native residents of each of the Native Villages to organize as Urban Corporations.

topic.name: State Sovereignty and Legal Protections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing state authority and protecting individual rights within the context of federal regulations and employment rights.

Document 1: authorizes a designated state official to submit to the head of a federal agency proposing a rule
commence a civil action against the agency on the grounds that the rule violates the Tenth Amendment.
Document 2: waives a state's sovereign immunity with respect to the enforcement of uniformed services members' employment or reemployment rights
provides a right to a jury trial in such cases.
Document 3: authorizes any state or local government or related official to file a motion to modify or terminate a federal consent decree
places the burden of proof with respect to such motions on the party originally filing the action.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, services, and regulations governing the United States Postal Service.

Document 1: Postmark Payment Act of 2012
deemed any payment required to be made on or before a prescribed date
Document 2: Community Post Office Relocation Act
authorizes the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to sell post office buildings
Document 3: directs the Postmaster General to issue a commemorative postage stamp
on Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis

topic.name: Missing Children and Crime Prevention
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving the reporting, recovery, and prevention of crimes involving missing children and violent crime.

Document 1: require federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies to certify compliance with reporting missing children
provide written information sheets to individuals reporting a missing child
Document 2: authorize the Attorney General to designate areas with high gang crime activity
provide community-based justice grants to state and local prosecutors
Document 3: assist law enforcement agencies to promote the quick recovery of a missing child
establish and maintain regional centers to provide training and information to law enforcement agencies

topic.name: Abortion Regulations and Fetal Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating abortion practices, particularly concerning fetal pain awareness and the treatment of fetal remains.

Document 1: This bill prohibits federal funding of any affiliate, subsidiary, successor, or clinic of Planned Parenthood Federation of America that receives compensation for facilitating the donation of fetal tissue from an abortion.

the Department of Justice must investigate whether Planned Parenthood Federation of America or any of its affiliates, subsidiaries, successors, or clinics has engaged in illegal activity pertaining to fetal tissue products.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require an abortion provider who knowingly performs an abortion of a pain-capable unborn child... to first: inform the woman of the probable age of the child; provide to the woman an Unborn Child Pain Awareness Brochure...
Creates an exception to save the life of the mother.
Document 3: This bill amends the federal criminal code to make it a crime to recklessly dispose of or abandon fetal remains in a landfill or in any navigable waters of the United States.
A violator is subject to a fine, up to three years in prison, or both.

topic.name: Safety Education and Preparedness
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing safety education and preparedness in various sectors, including fire safety and public health.

Document 1: establish a program to award grants to institutions of higher education for fire safety education programs
increasing fire safety awareness among enrolled students
Document 2: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to veterinary schools to improve public health preparedness
increasing the number of veterinarians in the workforce
Document 3: retain amounts collected for safety-related fines

topic.name: Government Policy and Support Programs
topic.description: The documents discuss various government initiatives aimed at providing support and benefits to specific groups, including military personnel, students with disabilities, and visa applicants.

Document 1: Expands the authority of the Secretary of Defense to accept travel benefits to facilitate the travel of members of the Armed Forces during leave from active duty
providing humanitarian support to members and eligible beneficiaries receiving care through the military health care system
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act regarding the visa waiver program
evaluate the security risks of aliens who enter the United States without an approved Electronic System for Travel Authorization verification
Document 3: Amends the school improvement program under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
ensure that the parents of students the state plans to assess using alternate assessments are involved

topic.name: Legislative Proposals for Public Services
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative bills aimed at improving public services, including healthcare, disaster assistance, and fisheries management.

Document 1: This bill amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to establish a five-year, population-based payment demonstration project
CMS may expand the project's duration and scope under specified circumstances.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers a tax credit, up to $2,000, for uncompensated travel expenses
incurred due to a mandatory evacuation of a disaster area.
Document 3: This bill directs the Mid-Atlantic Fishery Management Council to submit for approval by the Department of Commerce a modified fishery management plan
based on the best scientific information available; reflects changes in the distribution, abundance, and location of summer flounder.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Changes
topic.description: Legislative acts that modify existing federal laws and regulations across various sectors including law enforcement, land allotment for veterans, and education funding.

Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to transfer funds from other programs to the Impact Aid program.
